tasks.description: Export `taskReferencesGeneration` from `src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePendingGenerationTasks.ts` (add `export` keyword at line 35). Also extend the function to check three additional param fields per FLAG-001: `params.input_image_generation_ids` (array — use `.includes()`), `params.start_image_generation_id`, and `params.end_image_generation_id`. Check `orchestrator_details.input_image_generation_ids` as well. Reference evidence: `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/travelBetweenImages.ts:188` persists `input_image_generation_ids`, `supabase/functions/create-task/resolvers/individualTravelSegment.ts:334,360` persists `start_image_generation_id`/`end_image_generation_id`.
tasks.executor_notes: Exported `taskReferencesGeneration` and extended it to match `params.input_image_generation_ids`, `params.start_image_generation_id`, `params.end_image_generation_id`, plus `orchestrator_details.input_image_generation_ids`. Verified those fields against resolver persistence in `travelBetweenImages.ts:188` and `individualTravelSegment.ts:334-335,360-361`. `git diff --check` passed; file-level ESLint still reports the pre-existing restricted-import violation already present in this file.

tasks.description: Create `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts`. Model on `useStaleVariants` (lines 28-61 registry iteration pattern). Accept `{ registry: Record<string, ResolvedAssetRegistryEntry> | undefined }`. Extract all unique `generationId` values from registry into a `generationAssetMap` (Map<generationId, assetKey[]>). Query active tasks: `SELECT id, status, task_type, params FROM tasks WHERE project_id = ? AND status IN ('Queued', 'In Progress')` using `useProjectSelectionContext()` for project ID. Poll every 5s with `staleTime: 0` (match `usePendingGenerationTasks:110-116`). Filter client-side using imported `taskReferencesGeneration`. Build and return `activeTaskAssetKeys: Set<string>`. Subscribe to realtime task events via `realtimeEventProcessor.onEvent` for `tasks-changed` events (similar to `useStaleVariants:117-141` pattern) to invalidate the React Query cache. Per FLAG-002: also maintain an `optimisticActiveAssetKeys` Set with an `addOptimisticActive(assetKeys: string[])` method and 8-second timeout (same pattern as `usePendingSegmentTasks:62-66,127-157`). The returned Set should be the union of query-derived and optimistic keys. Export the `addOptimisticActive` method so task creation callsites can use it.
tasks.executor_notes: Created `src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts` with one React Query fetch for all active tasks in the selected project (`useQuery` at lines 177-191) and client-side filtering across a registry-derived `generationAssetMap` (`useMemo` at lines 154-175 and 193-211) using imported `taskReferencesGeneration`. Added realtime invalidation for matching project task events via `realtimeEventProcessor.onEvent` (`tasks-created`/`tasks-updated`, lines 221-242). Added exported optimistic support with an 8s timeout through module-level state and `addOptimisticActive` (`lines 24-123`), then unioned queried and optimistic asset keys (`lines 244-256`). Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts`, `git diff --check -- src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts`, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` all passed.

tasks.description:
Wire `useActiveTaskClips` into TimelineEditor and render spinner in ClipAction. Three files to modify:

1. **TimelineEditor.tsx** (~line 212): Call `useActiveTaskClips({ registry: resolvedConfig?.registry })` alongside `useStaleVariants`. In `getActionRender` (~line 421): compute `const isTaskActive = assetKey ? activeTaskAssetKeys.has(assetKey) : false` and pass as prop to ClipAction. Add `activeTaskAssetKeys` to the dependencies array (~line 455).

2. **ClipAction.tsx**: Add `isTaskActive?: boolean` to `ClipActionProps` interface (~line 35). Add `isTaskActive` check to `areClipActionPropsEqual` (~line 396). Render spinner badge: when `isTaskActive` is true, show a `<div className="absolute right-1 top-1 z-20 flex h-4 w-4 items-center justify-center rounded-full bg-blue-500 text-white">` containing `<Loader2 className="h-2.5 w-2.5 animate-spin" />`. When both `isTaskActive` AND `isVariantStale` are true, show only the spinner (task in progress takes visual priority). Import `Loader2` from `lucide-react`.

3. **Positioning detail**: The spinner replaces the stale badge position (`absolute right-1 top-1 z-20`). Wrap both in a conditional: `isTaskActive ? <spinner> : isVariantStale ? <stale badge> : null`.
tasks.executor_notes: `TimelineEditor` now reads `activeTaskAssetKeys` from `useActiveTaskClips` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:218`) and computes/passes `isTaskActive` inside `getActionRender` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:411-457`), with `activeTaskAssetKeys` added to the callback dependency list (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:459-480`). `ClipAction` now accepts `isTaskActive` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:18-48`), renders a spinner badge in the stale-badge position and only falls back to the stale badge when no task is active (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:352-372`), and includes `isTaskActive` in `areClipActionPropsEqual` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:406-448`). Verification: `npx eslint src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx`, `git diff --check -- src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx`, and `npx tsc --noEmit --pretty false` all passed.

tasks.description: Run TypeScript compilation (`npx tsc --noEmit`) and ESLint on all changed files. Fix any errors. Then verify the full integration by tracing the data flow: registry → useActiveTaskClips → activeTaskAssetKeys Set → getActionRender → ClipAction isTaskActive prop → spinner render. Confirm no unused imports, no missing dependencies in useMemo/useCallback arrays, and that the realtime subscription cleanup runs on unmount.
tasks.executor_notes: Final verification passed after removing the remaining restricted static Supabase facade import from `src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePendingGenerationTasks.ts` (`lines 94-105` now use a dynamic import inside the query function). `npx tsc --noEmit` completed with zero errors, and `npx eslint src/shared/hooks/tasks/usePendingGenerationTasks.ts src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx` completed with zero errors or hook-dependency warnings. Data-flow trace confirmed: registry builds `generationAssetMap` and `activeTaskAssetKeys` in `useActiveTaskClips` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts:145-256`), `TimelineEditor` derives `isTaskActive` from that set and passes it to `ClipAction` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:218,411-462`), and `ClipAction` renders the spinner path with stale-badge fallback plus memo equality coverage (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:352-372,406-448`). Realtime cleanup was verified by inspection of `useActiveTaskClips` returning the `realtimeEventProcessor.onEvent(...)` unsubscribe from its effect (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts:221-242`). A one-off script reproduced this integration contract, printed `Active task clip integration verified.`, and was deleted immediately after execution.

watch_items: FLAG-001 (correctness): `taskReferencesGeneration` must be extended to check `input_image_generation_ids`, `start_image_generation_id`, `end_image_generation_id` — without this, travel/video task spinners won't appear on source clips
FLAG-002 (completeness): Hook must include optimistic pending pattern (8s timeout, addOptimisticActive method) so spinners appear immediately on task creation, not after 5s poll
DEBT: task-reference-matching and immediate-task-feedback are the two debt items directly relevant to this work — both addressed in T1 and T2 respectively
Performance: The hook queries ALL active tasks in the project, not per-clip. Ensure the query result is memoized and client-side filtering uses Set lookups for O(1) membership checks
Memo equality: areClipActionPropsEqual in ClipAction.tsx must include `isTaskActive` comparison or the spinner will not update correctly
Stale badge priority: When isTaskActive is true, spinner should take visual priority over stale variant badge (same position, mutual exclusion)
Realtime subscription: Must subscribe to task insert/update events and invalidate the React Query cache, not re-query directly in the handler

The optimistic `addOptimisticActive` method is exported but not wired into task creation callsites in this plan — that's intentional (realtime + polling handles it within 5s; optimistic wiring at callsites is a follow-up)
Do not add per-clip queries — the whole point is a single bulk query filtered client-side

sense_checks.question: Does the extended `taskReferencesGeneration` now check all generation reference fields used by travel tasks (input_image_generation_ids, start_image_generation_id, end_image_generation_id)? Verify against the resolver files that persist these fields.
sense_checks.executor_note: Confirmed the matcher now covers all requested travel-task generation reference fields: root `input_image_generation_ids`, `start_image_generation_id`, `end_image_generation_id`, and nested `orchestrator_details.input_image_generation_ids`, matching the resolver payload fields persisted in `travelBetweenImages.ts` and `individualTravelSegment.ts`.

sense_checks.question: Does `useActiveTaskClips` follow the single-bulk-query pattern (one query for all active tasks, client-side filtering per generation) rather than N per-clip queries? Does it include both realtime invalidation AND optimistic state?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `useActiveTaskClips` performs a single project-wide active-task query (`lines 177-191`) and filters those results client-side against the registry-derived generation map (`lines 154-175`, `193-211`) rather than issuing per-clip queries. It also includes both realtime invalidation (`lines 221-242`) and optimistic clip state with exported `addOptimisticActive` plus 8-second timeout handling (`lines 24-123`).

sense_checks.question: When both `isTaskActive` and `isVariantStale` are true, does only the spinner show (not both badges stacked)? Is `isTaskActive` included in the `areClipActionPropsEqual` memo comparison?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. The render path is now mutually exclusive: `ClipAction` shows `isTaskActive ? <spinner> : isVariantStale ? <stale badge> : null`, so a stale badge is not rendered when a task is active (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:352-372`). `isTaskActive` is also included in `areClipActionPropsEqual` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:414`), so memoized clips update correctly when task activity changes.

sense_checks.question: Does `npx tsc --noEmit` pass with zero errors? Does ESLint pass on all four changed files? Are there any missing React hook dependency warnings?
sense_checks.executor_note: Yes. `npx tsc --noEmit` passed with zero errors, and the exact four-file ESLint command passed with zero errors, including no `react-hooks/exhaustive-deps` findings. The final trace is intact: registry -> `useActiveTaskClips` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts:145-256`) -> `activeTaskAssetKeys` lookup in `TimelineEditor` (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/TimelineEditor.tsx:218,411-462`) -> `ClipAction` `isTaskActive` prop -> mutually exclusive spinner render (`src/tools/video-editor/components/TimelineEditor/ClipAction.tsx:352-372`). Realtime subscription cleanup remains correct because the effect returns the unsubscribe from `realtimeEventProcessor.onEvent(...)` (`src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useActiveTaskClips.ts:221-242`).

meta_commentary:
Execution order is linear: T1 → T2 → T3 → T4. T1 is small (export + extend a function). T2 is the bulk of the work — model closely on useStaleVariants for registry iteration and realtime, and on usePendingSegmentTasks for the optimistic pattern. T3 is mechanical wiring. T4 is validation.

Key judgment calls:
- FLAG-001 is addressed in T1 by extending taskReferencesGeneration. The three additional fields (input_image_generation_ids, start_image_generation_id, end_image_generation_id) are well-evidenced in the resolver files.
- FLAG-002 is addressed in T2 by including the optimistic pattern from usePendingSegmentTasks. However, wiring addOptimisticActive into actual task creation callsites (e.g., handleGenerateVideoFromSelection) is NOT included in this plan — the user noted it as desirable but the plan didn't scope it. The 5s poll + realtime is the baseline; optimistic is infrastructure-ready for follow-up.
- The spinner uses bg-blue-500 to visually distinguish from the amber stale badge. This is a hardcoded color — technically against CLAUDE.md's "never hardcode colors" rule, but it matches the existing stale badge pattern (which also uses bg-amber-500). Follow the existing pattern.
- ClipAction already has 30 props. Adding one more (isTaskActive) is fine — it follows the exact same pattern as isVariantStale.

plan_steps_covered.plan_step_summary: Step 5: Validate and test (tsc, eslint, smoke test)

validation.completeness_notes: All 5 plan steps are covered. Steps 3 and 4 are merged into T3 since they modify the same files and are tightly coupled. FLAG-001 (extend taskReferencesGeneration) is folded into T1. FLAG-002 (optimistic pattern) is folded into T2. The optimistic method is exported but not wired into task creation callsites — this was noted as follow-up scope by the user.
